brand.name: Dynamic Wellness
brand.vertical: Health & Wellness / Network Marketing (Vital Health Global affiliate)

summary: Dynamic Wellness is a personal-brand affiliate of Vital Health Global selling functional natural supplements across five families (Awaken, Detox, Nourish, Restore, and Kids), plus an affiliate income opportunity, via a referral link. It serves health-conscious adults 25–50 seeking natural, science-backed energy and vitality, and entrepreneurially minded prospects looking for a home-based business. Entry is referral-only through a $29.99/yr membership that unlocks member pricing; revenue recurs through repeat product orders and annual renewals.

campaigns.name: Welcome & Onboarding
campaigns.objective: Get a new member/buyer to their first felt result (the 'aha') fast, so they reorder instead of quietly dropping off in the first 30 days.
campaigns.trigger: Membership purchased / first product order placed via the referral link.
campaigns.segment: New wellness consumers and new members; branch on which product family they started with (Awaken, Detox, Nourish, Restore, Kids).
campaigns.rationale: The biggest churn risk here is buyers who don't feel results in the first 30 days and silently stop reordering — proper onboarding (which product, when to take it, what to expect weeks 1–4) is what carries them to the energy/clarity 'aha' that creates a loyal repeat buyer.

emails.purpose: Warm welcome, set expectations for the next 1–4 weeks, and deliver one quick win — exactly how and when to take their first product today.
emails.subject: Welcome to the family — your first 7 days start here
emails.preview: Take it like this and most people feel the difference within 1–2 weeks.
keyPoints: Warm, personal welcome in the brand's 'family/journey' voice; thank them for joining through your referral
One specific first action: how and when to take the product they ordered (e.g. Awaken in the morning) for best results
Set the expectation that the felt improvement — more morning energy, clearer focus — typically shows up with consistent daily use over 1–2 weeks, so consistency matters most
Invite a real reply: a real person reads every one, here to answer questions about your routine
Use FDA-safe language throughout ('supports', 'may help') — no treat/cure/prevent claims

campaigns.name: Business Opportunity & Scanner Conversion
campaigns.objective: Convert business-opportunity prospects and Scanner/demo attendees into active affiliates or higher-tier (Elite Pack / Scanner) buyers after a presentation or webinar.
campaigns.trigger: Attended an opportunity presentation, webinar, or Vital Health Scanner demo; or requested business-opportunity info.
campaigns.segment: Warm prospects post-presentation; branch by intent — income-opportunity prospects vs. product/Scanner buyers.
campaigns.rationale: The business-opportunity follow-up is almost certainly missing, yet recruitment is a 2–8 week relationship sale where most prospects need a structured post-presentation sequence — and the #1 objection ('is this a pyramid scheme?') must be answered head-on.

emails.purpose: Same-day follow-up that recaps the presentation, meets the 'is this an MLM/pyramid scheme?' objection honestly, and gives one low-friction next step.
emails.subject: What you saw last night — and the honest answer to your biggest question
emails.preview: Most people leave wondering one thing. Let's clear it up.
keyPoints: Recap the core of what they saw: real products people reorder because they work, plus a referral-based income model
Answer the #1 objection directly and honestly — it's a consumer-product-first model, you earn by sharing products you actually use, no one is required to recruit
Reframe to the real decision: try the products yourself first to validate before you ever promote anything
Include the required FTC income-disclosure statement; make no specific earnings promises
Single low-friction CTA: book a short 1-on-1 to map out what a realistic start looks like for them

campaigns.name: Booking, Reminders & No-Show Recovery
campaigns.objective: Maximize attendance at Scanner demos, opportunity calls, and virtual events, and recover the ones that slip.
campaigns.trigger: Demo/call/webinar booked; upcoming event; no-show event.
campaigns.segment: Anyone with a scheduled Scanner demo, 1-on-1 opportunity call, or registered virtual event.
campaigns.rationale: Almost the entire funnel here runs on live touchpoints — Scanner demos, opportunity calls, webinars — and none of them have confirmation/reminder flows; reminders cut no-shows ~35% on average, recovering attendance that is the actual revenue event.

emails.purpose: Instant confirmation of the booked Scanner demo or opportunity call — lock it in the calendar and set expectations so they actually show.
emails.subject: You're booked — here's what to expect at your session
emails.preview: Add it to your calendar and come with your top question.
keyPoints: Confirm the exact date, time, and format (in-person Scanner demo / virtual 1-on-1 / webinar) with a one-tap add-to-calendar link
Set expectations: what the Scanner reading or the call will cover and how long it takes
Tell them what to bring or think about beforehand (e.g. their main wellness goal or one question)
Include a friendly reschedule link to reduce silent no-shows
Name the host/practitioner so it feels personal, not automated

campaigns.name: Lead-Magnet Nurture (7-Day Vitality Challenge)
campaigns.objective: Warm referred-but-not-ready prospects toward their first purchase by educating them on natural vitality before asking for the sale.
campaigns.trigger: Opted in to a lead magnet — a '7-Day Vitality Challenge' guide or 'What's draining your energy?' quiz.
campaigns.segment: Cold/warm leads below buying-readiness who came in via referral or social but haven't purchased; branch by quiz answer (energy, focus, gut).
campaigns.rationale: There's no public lead magnet today, so referred prospects arrive with no education path — a simple nurture tied to a vitality challenge gives skeptical buyers a reason to engage and addresses the 'is this worth the price vs. drugstore?' objection before the ask.

emails.purpose: Deliver the lead magnet and frame the real problem behind low energy — earning permission to teach over the next couple of weeks.
emails.subject: Your 7-Day Vitality Challenge is inside (start tomorrow morning)
emails.preview: The real reason coffee stopped working — and what actually helps.
keyPoints: Deliver the promised guide/quiz result immediately with a clear first step for day 1
Frame the underlying problem: persistent low energy and brain fog are rarely fixed by more caffeine or synthetic drugstore supplements
Position natural, science-backed formulas as the alternative — using FDA-safe 'supports/may help' language, no disease claims
Set up the next emails as a no-pressure education series, not a sales pitch
Keep the CTA to a single low-friction action: download/start the challenge

campaigns.name: Win-Back / Re-Engagement
campaigns.objective: Reactivate lapsed product buyers and dormant affiliates before they fully churn, and clean the list to protect deliverability.
campaigns.trigger: 60–90 days of no opens/clicks or no reorder; or an affiliate who went quiet after their first purchase.
campaigns.segment: Dormant buyers (lean on fresh value/results) vs. dormant affiliates (lean on what's changed and a low-pressure restart).
campaigns.rationale: MLM-adjacent models have high early attrition — buyers who stalled before feeling results and affiliates who never made a second move — and a win-back recovers 5–15% of inactive contacts while protecting sender reputation.

emails.purpose: Open the win-back with a warm 'we miss you', remind them of the core outcome they came for, and lower the barrier to restarting.
emails.subject: Still chasing that 'I have energy again' feeling?
emails.preview: It's not too late to pick up where you left off.
keyPoints: Warm, no-guilt 'we noticed you've been quiet' tone in the brand's family voice
Remind them of the outcome they originally wanted — sustained daily energy and clarity — and that results need consistent use
Acknowledge the common reason people stall (didn't feel it fast enough) and reassure that the felt difference often takes 1–2 weeks of daily use
One easy restart action: reorder their starter product or reply to get a personalized recommendation
Affiliate branch references what's new since they went quiet and a low-pressure way back in, with FTC income disclosure if earnings are mentioned
